dashboard.title: ESPHome - Device Builder
dashboard.subtitle: Create and make your smart devices and automations
dashboard.adopt_description: Taking control of {name} creates a local ESPHome configuration. From now on you manage updates from the dashboard — vendor-provided firmware updates won't apply automatically.
dashboard.adopt_encryption_hint: Generates a fresh key for the Native API. Recommended; required for Home Assistant integration.
dashboard.adopt_error_generic: Failed to take control of this device
dashboard.no_results_desc: No devices match "{query}". Try a different name or configuration file.
dashboard.delete_selected_desc: Are you sure you want to delete {count} device(s)? This will remove their configuration files and cannot be undone.
dashboard.delete_single_desc: Are you sure you want to delete "{name}"? This will remove its configuration files and cannot be undone.
dashboard.archive_selected_desc: Archive {count} device(s)? Their build files will be removed and you'll need to recompile from scratch when you restore them. You can find archived devices later from the menu (⋮) at the top right of the dashboard under "Archived devices".
dashboard.action_clean_failed: Failed to clean build files for "{name}"
dashboard.download_no_binaries: No firmware binaries found for "{name}" — compile first
dashboard.action_api_key_not_found: Unable to automatically extract API key. It may not be set. Open the configuration editor and look for the api: section with an encryption key.
dashboard.logs_method_title: How to get the logs for your device?
dashboard.logs_method_wireless_desc: Requires the device to be online
dashboard.logs_method_usb_local_desc: For devices connected via USB to this computer
dashboard.logs_method_usb_server: Plug into computer running ESPHome Dashboard
dashboard.logs_method_usb_server_desc: For devices connected via USB to the server
dashboard.logs_web_serial_unsupported: Web Serial is not supported in this browser
dashboard.install_method_title: How do you want to install the firmware?
dashboard.install_method_network_desc: Requires the device to be online
dashboard.install_method_usb_local_desc: For devices connected via USB to this computer
dashboard.install_method_usb_local_unsupported: Requires a browser with Web Serial support (e.g. Chrome)
dashboard.install_method_usb_server: Plug into computer running ESPHome Dashboard
dashboard.install_method_usb_server_desc: For devices connected via USB to the server
dashboard.install_method_web_download_desc: Requires a browser with Web Serial support (e.g. Chrome)
dashboard.install_method_manual_download_desc: Compile here and download the binary, then flash it manually with your preferred tool
dashboard.install_method_no_ports: No serial ports found. Make sure the device is connected to the server.
dashboard.table_status_unencrypted_tooltip: API traffic is unencrypted — anyone on the LAN can access this device
dashboard.table_status_encryption_pending_tooltip: Encryption is configured in the YAML but the device is still running plaintext — install the firmware to apply
dashboard.table_status_encryption_mismatch_tooltip: YAML enables encryption but the device is broadcasting plaintext — reinstall the firmware to apply
dashboard.drawer_config_hash_out_of_sync: Out of sync — flash to update
dashboard.drawer_version_out_of_sync: Out of sync — flash to update
dashboard.archive_desc: Archive "{name}"? Its build files will be removed and you'll need to recompile from scratch when you restore it. You can find archived devices later from the menu (⋮) at the top right of the dashboard under "Archived devices".
dashboard.action_archive_success_hint: Restore from the menu (⋮) at the top right under "Archived devices".
dashboard.delete_archived_desc: Are you sure you want to permanently delete the archived device "{name}"? This will remove its YAML and stored configuration. This cannot be undone.
dashboard.archived_dialog_desc: Devices you've archived are kept here so you can restore them later. Use Unarchive to put one back in your active device list, or Delete permanently to remove it for good.
dashboard.archived_dialog_empty: You haven't archived any devices yet.

command.try_reset_suggestion: It looks like you're having trouble with your build. If this keeps happening, try {action}.
command.show_secrets_tooltip: Reveal resolved !secret values in the validate output
command.show_logs_after_install_tooltip: Toggle whether to open device logs automatically after a successful install (like esphome run)
command.reset_success: Build environment reset. The next compile will redownload the toolchain.
command.reset_failed: Failed to reset the build environment.
command.queued_message: Another firmware task is running. This task will start when it finishes.

firmware.no_flashable_binary: No flashable binary available for this device. web.esphome.io supports ESP32 and ESP8266 only.
firmware.no_binaries: No binaries were produced for this device. Make sure the build completed successfully.
firmware.flash_failed: Failed to flash firmware to device.
firmware.chip_mismatch: Chip mismatch: detected {detected} but device expects {expected}.
firmware.web_download_done_body: Saved as {filename}. Open web.esphome.io on a computer that can connect to your device via USB and flash the file.
firmware.web_download_step_connect: Click Connect and pick your device's serial port
firmware.web_download_step_install: Choose Install and select the downloaded {filename}
firmware.binary_download_done_body: Saved as {filename}. Flash it to your device with your preferred tool.

serial.title: USB Device
serial.plug_in_desc: Plug your board into a USB port, then click the button below.
serial.chip_detected_desc: Create a new device for this board, or close to flash later.
serial.ready_to_flash: Ready to flash firmware via USB.
serial.not_supported: Web Serial is not supported in this browser. Use Chrome or Edge.
serial.no_firmware: No firmware binary found — compile the device first.

wizard.board_subtitle: Choose the ESP board you are using for this project.
wizard.no_boards_found: No boards found. Try a different search.
wizard.dont_know_board: I don't know what board I have
wizard.how_create: How would you like to create your project?
wizard.create_new_desc: A guided process to get you started with a starter kit or any other ESP boards
wizard.empty_config_desc: For manually writing or pasting a configuration.
wizard.drop_yaml: You can also drag and drop a .yaml file here
wizard.section_name_device_desc: Naming your project will show up in the ESPHome dashboard. Choose a recognisable, descriptive and memorable name. You can use component names or relevant words. You can edit this after you finish this project.
wizard.wifi_configuration_desc: How your device connects to your network.
wizard.import_general_error: Failed to import the config file. Please try again.
wizard.import_invalid_filename: The filename "{name}" can't be used as-is — it contains only characters that aren't allowed in a configuration filename. Rename the file to something with letters, digits, hyphens, or underscores and try again.

device.navigator_desc: Navigate, add and remove options to define your ESP device and its functionality.
device.section_core_desc: Your base functionality to allow your ESP board to run/function.
device.section_components_desc: You can add components after finishing your core configuration e.g. sensors.
device.section_automations_desc: You can set up automations once the core config and at least one component is added.
device.confirm_delete_section: Are you sure you want to delete the '{name}' section?
device.welcome_banner_body: A minimal default configuration has been applied. From here you can refine the core settings, add components like sensors or lights, and set up automations. Use the Device navigator on the left or the panels below to get started.
device.step_core_desc: The core configuration components are the base building blocks that let your board run ESPHome. They cover settings such as the device name, WiFi or Ethernet network, OTA updates, the API connection to Home Assistant, and optional add-ons like MQTT. Use the Device navigator on the left to view, edit or add core components.
device.step_components_desc: Components are the building blocks that bring your device to life — sensors, switches, lights, displays, buses, and more. Each one represents a piece of hardware on your board or a virtual entity exposed to Home Assistant. Use the Device navigator on the left to view, edit or add components.
device.step_automations_desc: Automations let your device react to events on its own — turning on a light when a button is pressed, sending a notification when a sensor changes, scheduling tasks, and so on. They run locally on the device. Use the Device navigator on the left to view, edit or add automations.
device.multi_value_empty: No items yet — click + to add one.
device.map_empty: No entries yet — click + to add one.
device.pin_input_only: Input only — can't be used as output
device.yaml_only_section: This section doesn't have a structured editor — edit it directly in the YAML pane on the right.
device.add_component_hidden_validation_error: Can't add this component: a hidden field has invalid input. Please report the fields listed below — they should be visible.
device.missing_dependencies_body: Configure these first, then come back to add this one:
device.return_to_after_dep_prefix: Adding a dependency. We'll bring you back to
device.add_automation_unavailable: Automation support is not yet available
device.unsaved_message: You have unsaved changes in this device. If you leave without saving, they will be lost.

validation.invalid_device_name: Only lowercase letters, digits, hyphens and underscores
validation.device_name_underscore: Underscores work but aren't valid mDNS hostnames — the device may not resolve on RFC-strict networks
validation.device_name_edge_hyphen: Names starting or ending with a hyphen aren't valid mDNS hostnames — the device may not resolve on RFC-strict networks

layout.feedback_menu: Found a bug? Have an idea?

feedback.title: Found a bug? Have an idea?
feedback.description: Help us improve ESPHome Device Builder by reporting issues or sharing your ideas.

command_palette.placeholder: Type a command — or / to search YAML content

yaml_search.no_match_yaml_preview: Try YAML search — found {count} match across your configs
yaml_search.no_match_yaml_preview_plural: Try YAML search — found {count} matches across your configs

settings.title: Settings
settings.language_desc: Choose the language used across the interface.
settings.theme_desc: Choose between light, dark, or follow your system preference.
settings.yaml_diff_desc: Show a diff button in the YAML editor to compare unsaved changes with the last saved version.
settings.show_yaml_diff_button_desc: Toggle on to see the YAML diff button in the editor toolbar. It lets you compare your unsaved changes against the last saved version.
settings.dark_mode_desc: Use dark colors for the interface

secrets.title: Secrets
secrets.desc: Manage sensitive values referenced in your device configurations with !secret
secrets.file_header:
# Secrets — store sensitive values here and reference them
# in your device configurations using !secret <key>
#
# Example:
#   wifi_ssid: "your-network-name"
#   wifi_password: "your-password"

auth.description: Sign in to manage your ESPHome devices.
auth.rate_limited: Too many failed attempts. Try again in {seconds}s.
auth.rate_limited_generic: Too many failed attempts. Try again later.

firmware_jobs.title: Firmware tasks
firmware_jobs.empty_desc: Compile, install, or clean jobs you start will show up here.
firmware_jobs.reset_confirm_message: This wipes the toolchain cache and platform/library installs. The next compile will be slow because everything has to re-download. Continue?
